sections.title: The passive (present simple, past simple, will)

content.subtitle: Form
content.description: be in the right form + past participle

content.subtitle: Active and passive
items: present simple: They always invite Grandma. → Grandma **is** always **invited**.
past simple: They invited Uncle Adrian. → Uncle Adrian **was invited**.
will: They **will / won't** invite the neighbours. → The neighbours **will / won't be invited**.

content.subtitle: Use
items: When the person responsible for the action is unknown: Our car **was broken into** last night.
When the person responsible for the action is unimportant or obvious: **Was** the package **delivered** this morning?

content.subtitle: Useful notes
content.intro: If you are unsure how to form a passive sentence, start by thinking of the **active** sentence first.
items: Look at the active sentence. The verb is *stole* and the object is *my sister's bike*.
The object of the active sentence (*my sister's bike*) becomes the subject of the passive sentence. *My sister's bike* …
Then we need the verb *be* in the same tense as the verb in the active sentence. Here, *stole* is past simple, so we need *was*. *My sister's bike was* …
Then we need the past participle of the verb in the active sentence. The past participle of *steal* is *stolen*. *My sister's bike was stolen* …
Finally, we finish the sentence in the right way. ***My sister's bike was stolen yesterday.***

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: When the verb in a passive sentence is a phrasal verb, don't forget to include the particle. They **picked up** the broken glass. → The broken glass was **picked up**.
Some verbs have irregular past participle forms (e.g. steal → stolen).

sections.title: A: Complete with the correct passive form
sections.instructions: Use the verbs in brackets in the present simple passive, past simple passive or will passive.

items.sentence: The invitations ...... (send) by email every week.
Our classroom ...... (paint) last summer.
The final results ...... (announce) tomorrow.
These shoes ...... (make) in Italy.
The windows ...... (not clean) yesterday.
...... the food ...... (serve) at 7 p.m.?
All homework ...... (check) by the teacher on Fridays.
My phone ...... (not find) after the party.

sections.title: B: Change active to passive
sections.instructions: Rewrite each sentence in the passive.

items.sentence: People speak English in many countries.
items.answer: English is spoken in many countries.

items.sentence: Someone stole my backpack yesterday.
They will open the new sports centre in June.
items.answer: The new sports centre will be opened in June.

items.sentence: They don't sell tickets online.
Did they invite Laura to the concert?
items.answer: Was Laura invited to the concert?

items.sentence: The mechanic fixed our car this morning.
items.answer: Our car was fixed this morning.

sections.title: C: Circle the correct form
sections.instructions: Choose the correct option in each sentence.

items.sentence: This museum **visited / is visited / was visiting** by thousands of tourists every year.
The homework **checked / was checked / is checking** last night.
Our new school website **will launch / will be launched / is launched** next week.
The dishes **are washed / wash / were washing** every evening.
The classroom door **wasn't close / wasn't closed / didn't closed** after the lesson.
**Is your parcel delivered / Is your parcel delivery / Does your parcel delivered** today?

sections.title: D: Complete with by or with
sections.instructions: Write by or with in each gap.

items.sentence: The picture was painted ...... my aunt.
The door was opened ...... a key.
The song was written ...... two students.
The room was cleaned ...... a new robot vacuum.
The cake was made ...... my brother for my birthday.
The message was sent ...... WhatsApp.

sections.title: E: Phrasal verbs in the passive
sections.instructions: Complete each sentence with the correct passive form of the phrasal verb in brackets.

items.sentence: The broken glass ...... (pick up) quickly after the accident.
All the chairs ...... (set out) before the guests arrived.
The lights ...... (turn off) at 10 p.m. every day.
The old bridge ...... (pull down) next year.
My name ...... (leave out) on the list by mistake.
The children ...... (pick up) at the station by their grandparents.

sections.title: F: Error correction
sections.instructions: Each sentence has one mistake. Write the correct passive form.

items.sentence: The windows **is** cleaned every morning.
My bike **were** stolen last weekend.
The winners **will announced** tonight.
**Is** the letters sent every Friday?
The room wasn't **tidy** by the students.
This app **use** by millions of people.
Our lunch ...... in the school canteen every day.
The old cinema ...... in 1980.
The final match ...... next Saturday.
The emails ...... yesterday because the internet was down.
Where ...... these bags made?
The classroom ...... after the meeting.

sections.title: H: Key word transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence so it means the same as the first.

items.sentence:
Someone cleans this office every evening.
This office ...... (CLEANED) every evening.

They delivered my order yesterday.
My order ...... (DELIVERED) yesterday.

They will finish the project next month.
The project ...... (FINISHED) next month.

Did someone invite Marco to the party?
...... (INVITED) to the party?

People don't use this road at night.
This road ...... (USED) at night.

Someone picked up my package this morning.
My package ...... (PICKED) this morning.
